variables: Variables are physical containers that store data like boxes
Variables can only hold one type of data
Changing a variable's value 'uses up' the old value forever
A variable name IS the data (confusing identifier with value)
loops: Loops always run an infinite number of times
Loops can only repeat the exact same action each time
A 'while' loop checks its condition constantly during the body, not just at the start of each iteration
For loops always count upward from zero
algorithms: Algorithms are only for computers, not humans
An algorithm must be written in code to be valid
There is only one correct algorithm for any given problem
A faster algorithm is always better regardless of memory usage
binary numbers: Binary is just another number system with no special meaning
Computers use binary because it's faster than decimal
Binary 10 is the same as decimal 10
Hexadecimal is a completely separate system unrelated to binary
data structures: Linked lists are always slower than arrays because they are 'linked'
Arrays can grow in size automatically without performance cost
A stack and a queue are basically the same thing because they both hold data
Hash tables always have O(1) lookup regardless of load factor
sorting: Bubble sort is the most efficient because it's the easiest to understand
Sorting only works on numbers, not text or complex objects
Quicksort is always O(n log n) regardless of the pivot choice
Merge sort uses less memory than quicksort because it's more organised
oop: An object is just a list of variables
Inheritance means the child class 'steals' code from the parent
Private variables are for security against hackers, not for encapsulation
Polymorphism means an object can be two things at the same time
recursion: Recursion is just a loop with a different name
A recursive function calls itself 'at the same time' (parallelism confusion)
Recursion will always eventually stop even without a base case
Recursion is always less efficient than iteration
time complexity: O(n^2) is always slower than O(n log n) for all inputs
Big O notation measures actual runtime in seconds
An O(1) algorithm is always fast in practice
The best algorithm is always the one with the lowest Big O class
graphs: A graph is the same thing as a chart
Trees are not a type of graph
BFS always finds the shortest path in a weighted graph
machine learning: Machine learning models 'understand' data the way humans do
More data always leads to a better model
A high training accuracy means the model will generalise well
Neural networks work exactly like the human brain
networks: The internet and the World Wide Web are the same thing
Data packets always travel the same route across a network
HTTPS means the website is trustworthy, not just encrypted
A firewall blocks all malicious traffic automatically

force: Force always causes motion (objects at rest have no forces)
Force is the same as energy
An object moving at constant speed must have a net force acting on it
If an object is not moving, no forces are acting on it
gravity: Gravity only affects objects on Earth
Heavier objects fall faster due to stronger gravity
There is no gravity in space (the 'vacuum' misconception)
Gravity is the strongest of the fundamental forces
energy: Energy is a physical substance that can be used up
Kinetic energy only comes from motion, not potential
Energy is 'lost' when a machine isn't 100% efficient
Cold is the absence of energy (rather than low thermal energy)
thermodynamics: Heat and temperature are the same thing
Cold is a type of substance that flows from cold to hot objects
The second law of thermodynamics means everything eventually breaks down into nothing
A perfectly insulated container means zero heat loss
waves: Sound can travel through a vacuum like light can
Transverse waves move matter in the direction of wave travel
A louder sound means a higher frequency
Light is purely a wave with no particle properties
electromagnetism: Electricity flows at the speed of light through wires
Current flows from negative to positive (confusing conventional and electron flow)
Magnets only attract or repel metal objects
An electric field exists only when current is flowing
momentum: Momentum and velocity are the same thing
A heavier object always has more momentum than a lighter one
In a collision, the total momentum always decreases due to energy loss
Impulse only matters for fast-moving objects
optics: We see objects because our eyes send light to them
Mirrors reverse left and right, but not up and down
Refraction only occurs when light passes from air into glass
A concave mirror always produces an inverted image
quantum mechanics: Quantum mechanics only applies to very small, very fast particles
The Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle is due to imperfect measuring instruments
Quantum superposition means a particle is in two places at once
Electrons orbit the nucleus in fixed circular paths like planets
circuits: Current is used up as it flows through a circuit
Voltage is the same as current
Adding more resistors in series makes the circuit brighter
A battery stores electricity (rather than chemical energy)
nuclear physics: Radioactivity is contagious and can spread from person to person
Nuclear fission and fusion are the same process in reverse
All radiation is equally dangerous regardless of type or dose
Atoms are destroyed in nuclear reactions
gravitational fields: Gravitational potential energy only exists near Earth's surface
Orbiting satellites have no gravity acting on them
A satellite in orbit must constantly fire its engines to stay up

dna: All DNA is genes (ignoring non-coding regions)
DNA is a static blueprint that never changes
Genes directly produce traits (ignoring gene expression complexity)
Humans have more genes than simpler organisms
cell division: Mitosis and meiosis produce identical outcomes
Cell division creates new DNA from scratch
Cancer is caused simply by cells dividing too fast (ignoring checkpoint failures)
All cells in the body divide at the same rate
evolution: Evolution is goal-directed and organisms evolve to become 'better'
Evolution is 'just a theory' with no empirical evidence
Humans evolved from chimpanzees (not from a common ancestor)
circulatory system: Veins carry oxygenated blood and arteries carry deoxygenated blood (always)
The heart pumps blood by squeezing it like a squeeze bottle
Blood is blue inside the body
The heart only pumps blood to the lungs
photosynthesis: Plants get their food from the soil
Plants only photosynthesise in sunlight and only respire at night
Oxygen is the main product plants use from photosynthesis
All plants photosynthesise at the same rate
nervous system: We use only 10% of our brain
Left-brained people are logical and right-brained people are creative
Reflexes are controlled by the brain, not the spinal cord
genetics: Dominant traits are more common in a population
Offspring receive exactly 50% of traits from each parent
Recessive traits skip generations and disappear
Identical twins have exactly the same DNA with no differences
immune system: A fever is always harmful and must be reduced immediately
Vaccines contain the actual disease-causing pathogen
Hand sanitiser is as effective as handwashing for all pathogens
ecology: Producers always have the most biomass in an ecosystem
Removing a predator from an ecosystem always increases biodiversity
Food chains are simple linear sequences in nature
Decomposers are less important than producers or consumers
hormones: Adrenaline only activates during extreme situations
Hormones work instantaneously like electrical signals
Insulin only matters for people with diabetes

derivatives: The derivative is the same as the slope of a straight line
f'(x) = 0 always means a maximum or minimum
Differentiation and integration are completely unrelated operations
The chain rule only applies to exponential functions
integration: Integration is just reverse differentiation with no geometric meaning
Definite and indefinite integrals are the same thing
The constant of integration is just a formality with no real meaning
Integration can only be applied to smooth, continuous functions
probability: The probability of an event increases if it 'hasn't happened in a while' (Gambler's Fallacy)
A 50% chance means it will definitely happen once in every two tries
Probability 0 means an event is impossible
Independent events cannot occur at the same time
matrices: The determinant gives the size of the matrix
Matrices are only useful in computer graphics
limits: A limit tells you the value of a function at that point
If a function is undefined at a point, its limit there is undefined too
Infinity is a number you can reach
L'Hopital's Rule can always be applied to any limit
statistics: The mean is always the best measure of central tendency
A p-value of 0.05 means there is a 5% chance the null hypothesis is true
A larger sample is always better regardless of sampling method
complex numbers: Imaginary numbers are not 'real' and have no practical applications
Complex numbers can be ordered on a number line
The modulus of a complex number is always a complex number
trigonometry: sin, cos, and tan are only ratios in right triangles, not functions
The unit circle is just a memorisation tool with no deeper meaning
Radians are more complicated than degrees and should be avoided
arcsin always returns the correct angle for any triangle problem
number theory: Zero is not an even number
Every number is either rational or irrational, nothing in between
Infinity plus one is larger than infinity
differential equations: Differential equations always have a neat analytical solution
The order of a differential equation refers to how many solutions it has
Initial conditions are optional extras, not essential for finding a unique solution
All differential equations model exponential growth or decay
